topics.title: Haushaltsökonomie
topics.description: Die Haushaltsökonomie beschäftigt sich mit der Entscheidungsfindung von Haushalten bei der Allokation ihrer Ressourcen.

flashcards.question: Was ist das Ziel eines Haushalts?
flashcards.answer: Das Ziel eines Haushalts ist die Maximierung seines Nutzens.

games.question: Was ist das Ziel eines Haushalts?
games.correct_answer: Das Ziel eines Haushalts ist die Maximierung seines Nutzens.
distractors: Das Ziel eines Haushalts ist die Minimierung seiner Ausgaben.
Das Ziel eines Haushalts ist die Maximierung seines Einkommens.
Das Ziel eines Haushalts ist die Minimierung seiner Bedürfnisse.

games.question: Matchen Sie die Begriffe
games.pair: Präferenzen:Vorlieben eines Haushalts;Nutzen:Zufriedenheit durch den Konsum von Gütern;Budget:Verfügbarer Betrag für den Konsum;Indifferenzkurven:Kurven gleichen Nutzens

topics.title: Unternehmensökonomie
topics.description: Die Unternehmensökonomie beschäftigt sich mit der Entscheidungsfindung von Unternehmen bei der Allokation ihrer Ressourcen.

flashcards.question: Was ist das Ziel eines Unternehmens?
flashcards.answer: Das Ziel eines Unternehmens ist die Maximierung seines Gewinns.

games.question: Was ist das Ziel eines Unternehmens?
games.correct_answer: Das Ziel eines Unternehmens ist die Maximierung seines Gewinns.
distractors: Das Ziel eines Unternehmens ist die Minimierung seiner Kosten.
Das Ziel eines Unternehmens ist die Maximierung seiner Produktion.
Das Ziel eines Unternehmens ist die Minimierung seiner Preise.

games.question: Matchen Sie die Begriffe
games.pair: Produktionsfunktion:Beziehung zwischen Input und Output;Kostenfunktion:Beziehung zwischen Input und Kosten;Erlösfunktion:Beziehung zwischen Output und Erlös;Gewinnfunktion:Beziehung zwischen Erlös und Kosten

topics.title: Mikroökonomie
topics.description: Die Mikroökonomie beschäftigt sich mit der Analyse von Märkten und der Entscheidungsfindung von Haushalten und Unternehmen.

flashcards.question: Was ist die Mikroökonomie?
flashcards.answer: Die Mikroökonomie ist die Analyse von Märkten und der Entscheidungsfindung von Haushalten und Unternehmen.

games.question: Was ist die Mikroökonomie?
games.correct_answer: Die Mikroökonomie ist die Analyse von Märkten und der Entscheidungsfindung von Haushalten und Unternehmen.
distractors: Die Mikroökonomie ist die Analyse von Volkswirtschaften.
Die Mikroökonomie ist die Analyse von Finanzmärkten.
Die Mikroökonomie ist die Analyse von Unternehmensstrategien.

games.question: Matchen Sie die Begriffe
games.pair: Markt:Ort des Güter- und Dienstleistungsverkehrs;Nachfrage:Güter und Dienstleistungen, die von Haushalten nachgefragt werden;Angebot:Güter und Dienstleistungen, die von Unternehmen angeboten werden;Gleichgewicht:Zustand, in dem Nachfrage und Angebot gleich sind

topics.title: Arbeitsmarkt und Arbeitsmarktpolitik
topics.description: Understanding labor market and labor market policy

flashcards.question: What is the main goal of labor market policy?

games.question: What is the main goal of labor market policy?

flashcards.question: What is the formula for the Auslastungsgrad?

games.question: What is the formula for the Auslastungsgrad?

flashcards.question: What is the main goal of the Agenda 2010?
flashcards.answer: Improving the framework conditions for growth and prosperity in Germany

games.question: What is the main goal of the Agenda 2010?
games.correct_answer: Improving the framework conditions for growth and prosperity in Germany

games.question: Match the terms
games.pair: Agenda 2010:German labor market reform;Hartz IV:German unemployment benefit;Mindestlohn:Minimum wage;Bundesagentur für Arbeit:Federal Employment Agency

flashcards.question: What is the main goal of the European Employment Strategy?
flashcards.answer: Coordinating employment policies among EU member states

games.question: What is the main goal of the European Employment Strategy?
games.correct_answer: Coordinating employment policies among EU member states

games.question: Match the terms
games.pair: Europäische Beschäftigungsstrategie:European Employment Strategy;Lissabon-Strategie:Lisbon Strategy;Armutsreduktionsziel:Poverty reduction goal;Freizügigkeit des Faktors Arbeit:Free movement of labor

flashcards.question: What is the main focus of medium-term economic analysis?

games.question: What is the main focus of medium-term economic analysis?
Match the terms
games.pair: Mittlere Frist:Medium term;Kurze Frist:Short term;Lange Frist:Long term;AS-AD-Modell:AS-AD model

flashcards.question: What is the main effect of inflation on the economy?
flashcards.answer: Reduces the purchasing power of money

games.question: What is the main effect of inflation on the economy?
games.correct_answer: Reduces the purchasing power of money
distractors: Increases the purchasing power of money
Has no effect on the economy

topics.title: Microeconomics Introduction
topics.description: Introduction to microeconomics, including the theory of households and firms

flashcards.question: What is the main goal of a household in microeconomics?

games.question: What is the main goal of a household in microeconomics?
Match the terms
games.pair: Household:Consumes goods and services;Firm:Produces goods and services;Market:Where buyers and sellers meet;Economy:System of production and distribution

topics.title: Theory of Households
topics.description: The theory of households, including budget constraints and indifference curves

flashcards.question: What is the budget constraint in microeconomics?
flashcards.answer: The limit on a household's spending, given its income and prices

games.question: What is the budget constraint in microeconomics?
games.correct_answer: The limit on a household's spending, given its income and prices
distractors: The minimum amount a household must spend
The maximum amount a household can borrow
The average amount a household saves

games.question: Match the terms
games.pair: Budget Constraint:Limit on household spending;Indifference Curve:Curve of equal utility;Utility:Satisfaction from consumption;Opportunity Cost:Value of next best alternative

topics.title: Theory of Firms
topics.description: The theory of firms, including production functions and cost functions

flashcards.question: What is the production function in microeconomics?
flashcards.answer: The relationship between inputs and outputs in production

games.question: What is the production function in microeconomics?
games.correct_answer: The relationship between inputs and outputs in production
distractors: The relationship between costs and revenues
The relationship between supply and demand
The relationship between prices and quantities

games.question: Match the terms
games.pair: Production Function:Relationship between inputs and outputs;Cost Function:Relationship between costs and output;Revenue Function:Relationship between output and revenue;Profit Function:Relationship between revenue and costs

topics.title: Market Equilibrium
topics.description: The concept of market equilibrium, including supply and demand

flashcards.question: What is the market equilibrium in microeconomics?
flashcards.answer: The point where supply equals demand

games.question: What is the market equilibrium in microeconomics?
games.correct_answer: The point where supply equals demand
distractors: The point where supply exceeds demand
The point where demand exceeds supply
The point where price equals cost

games.question: Match the terms
games.pair: Supply:Amount producers are willing to sell;Demand:Amount consumers are willing to buy;Equilibrium:Point where supply equals demand;Disequilibrium:Point where supply does not equal demand

topics.title: Introduction to Microeconomics
topics.description: Understanding the basics of microeconomics, including supply and demand, consumer behavior, and market structures.

flashcards.question: What is the primary goal of a consumer in microeconomics?

games.question: What is the primary goal of a consumer in microeconomics?
Match the terms
games.pair: Supply:Amount of a product that producers are willing to sell;Demand:Amount of a product that consumers are willing to buy;Market Equilibrium:Point at which supply and demand are equal;Opportunity Cost:Value of the next best alternative

topics.title: Consumer Theory
topics.description: Analyzing consumer behavior, including preferences, budget constraints, and utility maximization.

flashcards.question: What is the term for a consumer's preference for one good over another?

games.question: What is the term for a consumer's preference for one good over another?
Match the terms
games.pair: Budget Constraint:Limit on a consumer's spending;Indifference Curve:Graph of different combinations of goods that provide the same level of satisfaction;Utility Function:Mathematical representation of a consumer's preferences;Marginal Rate of Substitution:Rate at which a consumer is willing to trade one good for another

topics.title: Production and Cost
topics.description: Examining the production process, including inputs, outputs, and costs.

flashcards.question: What is the term for the relationship between the quantity of inputs and the quantity of outputs?

games.question: What is the term for the relationship between the quantity of inputs and the quantity of outputs?
Match the terms
games.pair: Fixed Cost:Cost that remains the same even if the quantity of output changes;Variable Cost:Cost that changes as the quantity of output changes;Marginal Cost:Change in total cost resulting from a one-unit increase in output;Average Total Cost:Total cost divided by the quantity of output

topics.title: Perfect Competition
topics.description: Understanding the characteristics of perfect competition, including many buyers and sellers, free entry and exit, and homogeneous products.

flashcards.question: What is the term for a market structure in which many firms produce a homogeneous product?

games.question: What is the term for a market structure in which many firms produce a homogeneous product?
Match the terms
games.pair: Perfect Competition:Market structure with many firms and free entry and exit;Monopoly:Market structure with one firm;Monopolistic Competition:Market structure with many firms and differentiated products;Oligopoly:Market structure with a few firms
